etymology.description: Original pictogram (oven) -> oven as 田 (pictogram) -> phonetic 虍(虍) + semantic 田(田, 'oven') + semantic 皿(皿, 'vessel') -> current form
etymology.interpretation: 盧(盧) (lú, 'black') originally depicted an oven, with 虍(虍) added for the sound and 田(田) + 皿(皿) emphasizing its use as a vessel or container. The character evolved to its present form, where the oven sense is now defined by 爐(爐) (lú, 'stove').
etymology.simplification: Simplified to 卢(盧) by using a cursive shorthand.

examples.chinese: 盧是一個古老的姓氏(盧是一個古老的姓氏)。
examples.pinyin: Lú shì yī gè gǔlǎo de xìngshì.

examples.chinese: 盧花园里种满了各种各样的花卉(盧花園裡種滿了各種各樣的花卉)，游客络绎不绝(遊客絡繹不絕)。
examples.pinyin: Lú huāyuán lǐ zhòng mǎn le gèzhǒng gè yàng de huāhuì, yóukè luòyìbùjué.
examples.english: Lú Garden is filled with various kinds of flowers, and tourists come in an endless stream.

examples.chinese: 在《白马非马》这篇文章中(在《白馬非馬》這篇文章中)，盧植是一个逻辑的反例(盧植是一個邏輯的反例)。
examples.pinyin: Zài 《Báimǎ fēi mǎ》 zhè piān wénzhāng zhōng, Lú Zhí shì yī gè luójí de fǎnlì.
examples.english: In the article 'A White Horse is Not a Horse,' Lú Zhí is an example of logical contradiction.
